_comment: Global market assumptions shared across ALL profiles. No withdrawal sequences here — those are per-profile in economic.json.

overrides._comment: Years 1-5: conservative rebalancing, standard cash reserve
Years 6-15: slightly relaxed capgain limit as brokerage grows
Years 16-30: reduced cash reserve months, higher capgain limit

readme.purpose: Global market behavior settings shared across ALL profiles. Overrides section applies year-range-specific changes. Profile economic.json is merged on top for profile-specific differences — profile values win on any conflict.

bad_market.drawdown_threshold: Portfolio decline fraction from peak that triggers bad-market mode. E.g. 0.15 = 15% below peak switches to bad-market withdrawal sequencing and suppresses rebalancing.
bad_market.use_recession_flag: true = also trigger bad-market mode if the stochastic model sets a macro recession flag, even if drawdown threshold has not yet been crossed.
bad_market.p10_signal_enabled: true = also trigger bad-market mode when the cross-sectional P10 annual return across all paths is below p10_return_threshold_pct.
bad_market.p10_return_threshold_pct: P10 return level (%) below which bad-market mode triggers.

cash_reserve.months: Months of withdrawal target kept as liquid cash buffer.
cash_reserve.allow_dip_below_reserve: false = never let cash fall below the reserve floor.
cash_reserve.use_cash_only_in_bad_markets: true = only draw from cash reserve during bad markets.

rebalancing.global_drift_threshold: Maximum allowed drift as a fraction of target weight before rebalancing triggers.
rebalancing.require_cash_above_reserve_to_rebalance: true = do not rebalance if cash is at or below reserve.
rebalancing.suppress_in_bad_market: true = pause all rebalancing during bad-market periods.
rebalancing.brokerage_capgain_limit_k: Maximum capital gains in $k per year realized from brokerage rebalancing.

withdrawals.shock_scaling_enabled: true = scale down take-home withdrawals during bad markets.
withdrawals.drawdown_threshold: Portfolio decline fraction at which scaling begins.
withdrawals.min_scaling_factor: Minimum fraction of target withdrawal paid out. E.g. 0.65 = never below 65% of planned.

scale_curve_options.linear: Take-home scales linearly from 100% at threshold to min_scaling_factor at full drawdown.
scale_curve_options.poly: Polynomial scaling. scale_poly_alpha > 1 = gentle then sharp.

withdrawals.use_recession_flag: Also scale withdrawals when recession flag is set.
withdrawals.makeup_enabled: true = in good years after a scaled-down year, recover a portion of missed take-home.
withdrawals.makeup_ratio: Fraction of cumulative missed withdrawal recovered per good year.
withdrawals.makeup_cap_per_year: Maximum makeup as fraction of that year's target.
withdrawals.upside_scaling_enabled: false = withdrawals never exceed amount_k. true = scale up when portfolio outperforms.
withdrawals.upside_outperformance_threshold: Portfolio outperformance fraction above which upside scaling begins.
withdrawals.upside_max_factor: Maximum upside withdrawal as a multiple of amount_k.

excess_income_policy.purpose: Controls what happens when external income (W2, rental, SS, misc) net after-tax EXCEEDS the withdrawal_schedule target. The withdrawal target is reduced by net income — this policy handles the surplus.

surplus_policy_options.reinvest_in_brokerage: Route surplus to the first brokerage account. Grows the portfolio — best for accumulators still building wealth.
surplus_policy_options.spend: Treat surplus as additional spending. No portfolio impact — best for retirees who want to spend their full income.

excess_income_policy.income_offset_tax_rate: Approximate effective tax rate applied to gross income to estimate net take-home for portfolio offset. Default 0.30 (30%). This is a planning approximation — actual taxes are computed exactly by the tax engine. Set higher (e.g. 0.40) for high-income working years, lower (e.g. 0.20) for retirement years. Phase 2 will use exact computed taxes.
excess_income_policy.note: The withdrawal_schedule.json specifies the after-tax PORTFOLIO take-home target. When W2/rental/SS net income already covers living expenses, the simulator draws nothing from the portfolio for those years. This is the correct modeling of real behavior: a $450K earner with $150K expenses does not drain their IRA while working.

readme.overrides_note: Time-bounded only. Each override applies for its years range then reverts to defaults.
readme.core_inflation_pct: Long-run core inflation assumption (%). Fallback when inflation_yearly.json doesn't cover a year.
